nativeRange.habitat: The species-level Ludwigia palustris occurs widely in marshes, ditches, slow streams, and the muddy margins of ponds and ditches across temperate and subtropical regions. The 'Super Red' cultivar is a Tropica-bred form rather than a wild population.

decorPreferences: bright lighting for deepest red coloration

summary: Ludwigia Palustris Super Red is a compact cultivar of Ludwigia palustris bred for reliable red coloration and smaller growth than standard Ludwigia varieties. It is widely considered one of the easiest red stem plants to color up, responding well to moderate light, CO2, and iron dosing.
careNotes: Ludwigia Palustris Super Red is a cultivar of Ludwigia palustris selected for compact growth and reliable red coloration. It is widely regarded as one of the easiest red stem plants for newer aquascapers to color up successfully. Lighting and CO2 are the main levers for achieving the plant's characteristic deep red: the plant survives under low light without CO2 supplementation but will tend toward gold or olive tones rather than crimson. To push the deepest red coloration, medium-to-high light levels and CO2 injection are recommended. Keeping nitrate low — around 5 to 10 ppm — while dosing iron and micronutrients generously is widely cited among aquascapers as the most reliable approach to driving red pigmentation. The compact 'Super Red' grows noticeably smaller than standard Ludwigia palustris or Ludwigia repens, making it suitable for nano aquariums and midground placements as well as background hedges. A nutrient-rich substrate such as aquasoil produces the best results; root tabs can supplement nutrition when the plant is grown in inert gravel. Regular trimming encourages lateral branching, resulting in a denser, bushier form over time.
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: cut the top of a mature stem and replant the cutting directly into the substrate, where it will quickly root. Pinching off the apical bud encourages the mother stem to produce multiple lateral side shoots, which can be trimmed off and replanted in turn for rapid mass propagation. Floating cuttings will also root in the water column before planting.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Used for cultivar identity (Tropica-bred form), height (10-30+ cm), growth rate (medium), light (low) and CO2 (low) requirements, care difficulty (easy), placement (stem plant - mid/background), and propagation (cuttings produce side shoots, cut shoots re-root quickly). additional.name: KeepFishKeeping
additional.notes: Confirms Tropica cultivar origin, 2-4 cm wide × 10-30 cm tall per stalk, lateral side-shoot growth on trimming, intense red color with strong light and CO2, easier to colour up than L. repens 'Rubin'.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Confirms family Onagraceae, height 7-19" (~18-48 cm), care difficulty (easy), light moderate/high for color, CO2 recommended, fast growth, background placement. Notes color shifts gold-orange-red depending on conditions, and recommends aquasoil substrate.

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Confirms family Onagraceae, height 20-50 cm, fast growth, medium light, CO2 not required, temperature 18-28 °C, pH 5-7, kH 0-14, care easy, placement middle/background or nano accent. Describes it as 'the reddest Ludwigia'.
